chapter_title.en: Chapter on Whose Testimony Must Be Rejected and Whose Testimony Must Be Accepted

text: 3302 - وَ رَوَى عَبْدُ اَللَّهِ بْنُ اَلْمُغِيرَةِ عَنْ أَبِي اَلْحَسَنِ اَلرِّضَا عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "مَنْ وُلِدَ عَلَى اَلْفِطْرَةِ وَ عُرِفَ بِالصَّلاَحِ فِي نَفْسِهِ جَازَتْ شَهَادَتُهُ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3302 - And it was narrated by Abdullah ibn Al-Mughirah from Abu Al-Hasan Imam Ali ibn Musa Ar-Ridha (as), who said:
"Whoever is born upon the natural disposition (fitrah) and is known for his righteousness in himself, his testimony is valid."
